mechanism_narrative: A 5-month growth acceleration in the US GDP signals increased consumer and business confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in growth acceleration periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the industrial production index signals reduced supply chain bottlenecks, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in supply chain easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month rise in the unemployment rate signals a tightening labor market, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 4-month increase in payroll employment signals increased labor market participation, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in labor market growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month rise in the housing starts index signals increased demand for residential construction, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in housing demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 4-month increase in real disposable personal income signals increased consumer spending power, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in consumer spending periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in the capacity utilization rate signals reduced supply chain bottlenecks, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in supply chain easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
